name: baseline
_comment: Baseline Reference Case - Conservative assumptions for IRP planning

_parameters.name: Scenario identifier used in outputs and comparisons
_parameters.forecast_horizon: Number of years to project forward (10 years = 2025-2035)
_parameters.housing_growth_rate: Annual housing unit growth rate (1% per year, conservative)
_parameters.electrification_rate: Annual rate of gas-to-electric fuel switching (2% per year)
_parameters.efficiency_improvement: Annual equipment efficiency improvement rate (1% per year)
_parameters.weather_assumption: Weather scenario: 'normal' uses 30-year normals, 'warm' reduces HDD, 'cold' increases HDD
_parameters.end_use_scope: List of end-uses to simulate. Current scope: ['space_heating']. Future: add water_heating, cooking, clothes_drying, fireplace, other
_parameters.max_premises: Maximum premises to simulate (0 = all). Set to 1000-5000 for quick testing
_parameters.vectorized: Use fast vectorized simulation (true) or row-by-row loop (false)

_notes: This baseline scenario represents a reference case with conservative assumptions.
Housing growth of 1% aligns with historical NW Natural service territory trends.
Electrification of 2% reflects current policy and market adoption rates.
Efficiency improvements of 1% account for natural equipment replacement with newer, more efficient models.
Results are compared against NW Natural's 2025 IRP UPC forecast (648 therms baseline).
end_use_scope controls which end-uses are simulated. Current scope is space_heating only.
Set max_premises to a small number (e.g., 1000) for quick testing runs.
Set vectorized to true for production runs (100x faster than row-by-row).
